title: quercus ravenscroftiana
intro: Quercus ravenscroftiana, also known as the Ravenscroft oak, is a species of tree in the beech family (Fagaceae). It is primarily recognized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus ravenscroftiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Quercus ravenscroftiana can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for topical and internal use, although its availability and usage are largely limited and require further research.
